title: Interactive Stock and Flow Sandbox
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Hands-on simulation where students manipulate inflow and outflow rates to explore accumulation, depletion, and dynamic equilibrium in ecological systems

educational.topic: Stock and Flow Dynamics in Ecological Systems
learningObjectives: Construct a stock and flow diagram for a simple ecological system and predict how the stock changes over time
Explain the concept of dynamic equilibrium using simulation evidence
Apply stock-and-flow thinking to multiple ecological contexts

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Hands-on manipulation with ecological scenario presets
keyQuestions: What happens to a stock when inflow exceeds outflow?
What conditions produce dynamic equilibrium in an ecological system?
How does the same stock-and-flow pattern appear in different ecological systems?
What real-world factors control the inflow and outflow of carbon in the atmosphere?
commonMisconceptions: Students often think equilibrium means nothing is happening, rather than balanced rates
Students may assume stocks change instantly when rates change, not understanding the gradual accumulation process
Students sometimes confuse the stock level with the flow rate
assessmentOpportunities: Have students predict stock behavior before running the simulation, then compare with results
Ask students to design their own ecological stock-and-flow scenario
Students explain how changing one rate in a real ecosystem would affect the stock over time
